pageDescription: Estimate your blood alcohol content (BAC) based on drinks consumed, weight, gender, and time

bacFormula.label: BAC Formula
bacFormula.standard: BAC = (Alcohol Consumed in grams / (Body Weight in grams × Gender Constant)) × 100 - (Hours Elapsed × Metabolism Rate)

results.legalLimitInfo: Legal driving limit in most US states: 0.08%

criticalWarningsList.notExact: This calculator provides estimates only and may not reflect actual BAC
criticalWarningsList.impairment: Even small amounts of alcohol can impair driving ability

importantNotesList: BAC is affected by food consumption, medication, and individual metabolism
Women typically have higher BAC than men for the same amount of alcohol
Never drink and drive - use a designated driver or ride service
If you suspect alcohol poisoning, seek immediate medical attention

messages.enterMeasurements: Enter your information to estimate your blood alcohol content

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator provides estimates only and should not be used to determine if it is safe to drive. Many factors affect BAC that are not accounted for in this calculation. Always err on the side of caution and never drive after drinking alcohol.

seekHelp.title: Emergency Help
seekHelp.content: If you suspect alcohol poisoning (confusion, vomiting, slow or irregular breathing, blue-tinged skin, low body temperature, or unconsciousness), call emergency services immediately. Alcohol poisoning can be fatal.

errors.drinks: Please enter a valid number of drinks
errors.hoursDrinking: Please enter valid hours of drinking
errors.hoursElapsed: Please enter valid hours since first drink

tooltips.weight: Your current body weight affects BAC
tooltips.hoursDrinking: Duration over which drinks were consumed
tooltips.hoursElapsed: Time since first drink was consumed

enter_your_information_and_alcohol_consu: Enter your information and alcohol consumption details
required_for_watson_body_water_calculati: Required for Watson body water calculation
Required for Watson body water calculation
time_elapsed_since_your_first_alcoholic_: Time elapsed since your first alcoholic drink
ag_alcohol_grams_r_distribution_ratio_ma:
A_g = alcohol grams, r = distribution ratio (male: 0.68, female: 0.55), β = 0.017/hour
                        elimination
enter_your_information_and_drinks_then_c: Enter your information and drinks, then click
this_bac_calculator_provides_an_estimate:
This BAC calculator provides an estimate only. Actual BAC may vary based on individual
                            factors. Do not drink and drive. Always use designated drivers or alternative
                            transportation.
blood_alcohol_concentration_bac_measures:
Blood Alcohol Concentration (BAC) measures the amount of alcohol in your bloodstream as a
                        percentage. This calculator uses the Watson formula for more accurate Total Body Water
                        calculation compared to traditional Widmark equation.
watson_uses_age_height_weight_and_gender: Watson: Uses age, height, weight, and gender for precise TBW
widmark_uses_simple_gender_constants_les: Widmark: Uses simple gender constants (less accurate)
formula_bac_ag_r_wkg_10_t_81: Formula: BAC(%) = A_g ÷ (r × W_kg × 10) - β × t
the_liver_eliminates_alcohol_at_approxim:
The liver eliminates alcohol at approximately 0.017% BAC per hour (updated from older 0.015
                          rate), regardless of body size or drinking experience.
this_calculator_is_for_educational_purpo:
This calculator is for educational purposes only. Never rely on
                          BAC estimates to determine if you're safe to drive.
